# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# Set default database connection settings
host_based_authentication:
  hosts:
    localhost:
      all:
        method: trust
      # Allow admin user to connect from anywhere with password authentication
      admin: hostssl   0.0.0.0/0       md5        app-admin

    localnet:
      all:
        method: trust
      # Allow app and readonly users to connect from their specific IP addresses
      app:   host     192.168.1.100/32  password    app-user
      readonly: hostssl 172.16.3.200/32  md5        readonly-user

# Enable IPv4 connections only
listen_addresses = 'localhost, localnet'

# Set default role to allow local connections
local all all trust

# Set default connection method for hosts
host    all         all            localhost      trust
